name: fs-windows-relative-canon
note: Windows regression guard for the `\?\` verbatim-prefix strip (matcher/path.rs strip_verbatim_prefix). A `./`-relative project policy is compiled through the REAL compiler → std::fs::canonicalize returns a `\?\C:\...\proj` verbatim path → strip_verbatim_prefix must yield a plain `C:\...\proj` so the fully-literal grant lands as an inheritable ACE. If it regresses (the `?` re-reads as a glob metachar → the grant is mis-classified as an unenforceable embedded-glob and DROPPED), the AppContainer child is denied its OWN project dir and the two ALLOW cases below go red. The outside read + its relaxed negative control keep the guard from being vacuous. This is the INTENTIONAL, named guard for the never-green-leg bug; macOS/Linux exercise the same `./` compile incidentally via fs-read-confine/fs-write-confine, so this case is Windows-scoped.
